evals.prompt: Turn my 3rd grade fractions lesson into slides for tomorrow — about 7 slides.
evals.expected_output: A slide outline (one idea per slide, gradual-release flow, visuals with alt text, at least one CFU) aligned to the real Grade-3 standard, then rendered to .pptx via the pptx skill, with the metadata block. Should pass quality-review.
assertions: Follows a gradual-release flow with one idea per slide
Aligned to a real, cited Grade-3 CCSS fractions standard
Includes visuals with alt text and at least one check-for-understanding slide
Renders the .pptx using the pptx skill (does not hand-roll the format)
Includes the metadata block with human_review_required: true

evals.prompt: Make a short review deck to warm up my 7th graders before their ratios quiz.
evals.expected_output: A review/warm-up deck with item-per-slide prompts and reveal-answer slides, aligned to real Grade-7 CCSS ratio/proportion standards.
assertions: Uses item-per-slide prompts with reveal-answer slides
Aligned to real Grade-7 CCSS ratios/proportional-relationships standard(s)

evals.prompt: I need a parent-night presentation explaining how we teach reading in kindergarten.
evals.expected_output: A family briefing deck in plain language, no jargon, no real student data, with clear visuals.
assertions: Plain-language, jargon-free slides appropriate for families
Contains no real student data (placeholders only)
One idea per slide with supporting visuals
